div.head: Memorandum by the Adviser on Political Relations (Hornbeck)
div.p: The papers here attached Infra. were handed to me by the Chinese AmbassadorHu Shih. this afternoon.

The Ambassador said that he was giving me this informally in order to inform us of action which the Chinese Government is contemplating taking at the May session of the Council of the League of Nations. He said that he realized that the hope expressed that the American Government will associate itself with the League in adopting “similar
measures” and will join Great Britain, France and the Soviet Union in making a joint or parallel declaration reaffirming points specified is an expression of wish rather than of expectation. He hoped, however, that the American Government would move in the general direction contemplated and suggested to the powers in the Chinese Government’s outline and expression of request and hope.
